---
title: "Disposing Of Records Past Retention"
---

---
title: "Disposing of Records Past Retention"
source: "https://support.hyland.com/r/Current/Hyland-Governance/amh1748512003058"
section: "Hyland Governance"
---

When a record exceeds its retention period, depending on the defined disposition it is removed automatically or manually. If the disposition is set to Purge records must be removed manually by the Record Manager. The Record Manager is responsible for overseeing the organization, classification, and retention of records to ensure compliance with governance rules.

To remove a record that has passed its retention period:

1.  Open a  Governance  tab in Workspace.
2.  In Governance filters, find the Status filter.
3.  Select Reached Disposition.
4.  Click Apply. The search results are displayed.
5.  Select the records you want to remove.
6.  Click the Delete icon that is on the right above the search results.   ![Delete icon](data:image/png;base64,)
7.  Click Delete to confirm deletion.
8.  Repeat the action with the other records you want to delete.  Note: Bulk selection is not available.
